narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection stings—dreaming of law school only to have your application turned away because a model sees a 64% chance you'll struggle with the bar exam feels deeply unfair, but let me walk you through the story it told about your path, based on the details you shared. Picture your journey starting strong in Year 1, landing you in the 5th decile among your peers, but by Year 3, that slipped to the 3rd decile, and that downward trend became the model's biggest red flag, whispering loudest that challenges might pile up when the stakes get higher. Your LSAT score of 24 reinforced this, painting a picture of hurdles in tackling the intense reasoning the bar demands, pulling the prediction firmly toward difficulty. Even with your upper-middle family income, which the model saw as a potential buffer but still tilted against smoother sailing, it couldn't outweigh those academic dips. Your Asian background and undergraduate GPA of 2.4 added layers to this narrative, suggesting a profile where persistence alone might not bridge the gaps the model has seen falter before. Going full-time, while ambitious, echoed patterns of overload in similar stories, nudging the outlook lower still. Your gender was the one bright thread, gently pulling toward success in the model's eyes, like a quiet vote of confidence. Together, these pieces wove a tale of a capable student whose trajectory showed more friction than momentum, leading the school to pause. It's not the full you—just patterns from thousands of paths like yours—but it highlights where targeted growth, like boosting those later deciles or LSAT prep, could rewrite your story for the next opportunity. You've got the drive; now channel it to shift this script. Keep pushing—you're closer than it feels.
